questionHtml:
Molten zinc chloride undergoes electrolysis in an electrolytic cell at 450 °C.

Deduce the half-equations for the reaction at each electrode.
markschemeHtml:
Cathode (negative electrode):

Zn2+ + 2e− → Zn (l) ✔

 

Anode (positive electrode):

2Cl− → Cl2 (g) + 2e−
OR
Cl− → ½ Cl2 (g) + e− ✔

meta.title: Molten zinc chloride undergoes electrolysis in an electrolytic cell at 450 °C. Deduce the half-equations for the reaction at each electrode.
